description: Predicting continuous values is regression (e.g., house price, temperature). Predicting discrete categories is classification (e.g., 'spam' or 'not spam').
Calculates the average of the absolute differences between predicted and actual values. Provides a straightforward measure of how much, on average, predictions deviate from the truth.
The ratio of correctly predicted observations to the total observations in a classification model. While intuitive, it can be misleading if the dataset is highly imbalanced.
The proportion of positive identifications that were actually correct. Calculated as True Positives divided by the sum of True Positives and False Positives, focusing on the cost of 'false alarms'.
Measures a binary classifier's ability to distinguish between classes across all possible thresholds. A higher AUC indicates better prediction of true positives while minimizing false positives.
Data drift: statistical properties of input features change over time. Model drift (concept drift): relationship between input features and target variable changes, degrading predictive power.
A discrepancy between model performance during training and in production, often caused by using different data pipelines or features at inference time than during training.
A repository to store, update, retrieve, and share machine learning features. Ensures consistency between training and real-time inference by providing a single source of truth for feature data.

Automatically monitors machine learning models in production and alerts when deviations in model quality are detected. Can identify issues like data drift, concept drift, and changes in feature attribution.

Detects potential bias in datasets and models, and provides model explainability. Helps developers understand how individual features contribute to a model's prediction using methods like SHAP values.
A fully managed service making foundation models from leading AI startups and Amazon available via API. Allows building and scaling generative AI applications without managing infrastructure.

A preprocessing technique converting categorical variables into a binary vector representation. Each unique category becomes a new column with a 1 or 0, ensuring no numerical ranking between categories.
An individual measurable property or characteristic of a phenomenon being observed. In a dataset, features typically represent the columns (input variables) used by the model to make predictions.
Using domain knowledge to select, modify, or create new variables from raw data to improve model performance. Includes tasks like scaling, encoding, and creating interaction terms.
A centralized management layer for machine learning features supporting both batch and online applications. Helps teams share and reuse features across projects while maintaining data consistency.
A fully managed data labeling service to build highly accurate training datasets. Offers built-in labeling workflows and supports human labelers through vendors or private workforces.
A crowdsourcing marketplace for outsourcing tasks to a distributed workforce. In ML, frequently used for manual data labeling, cleaning, and validation tasks.
